Could you please advise what might be missing or if there’s something else I need to check?

With correct permissions you can open this also with a double-click.

entry.title: Re: SLT logging table not getting deleted /1CADMC/<DIGITS>
entry.content: I believe, If this logging table belongs to a deactivated configuration or a dropped replication, confirm that it's no longer needed. Double-check with BASIS or the application team before deletion. Since 1.5 billion entries is huge, coordinate with your BASIS/DB team.

entry.title: Re: SLT logging table not getting deleted /1CADMC/<DIGITS>
entry.content: If it's not needed, consider for a cleanup.

entry.title: MRP Run for FG Material With strategy 40 and strategy 50.
entry.content:
While  MRP run for the FG material with Strategy 40, it considering both SFG and RM and creates Planned orders and Purchase requisitions.  But for the same  FG material, if maintained Strategy 50, it does not considering the SFG and RM.  After creating demand in MD61. Am I missing something? Using Req type VSF for 40 and VSE for 50 while creating demand in MD61.

Maintained

MRP Type - FG, SFG, RM = PD.

Strategy Group - SFG, RM - Nil,  for FG - 40/50.

Ind/Coll requirement - SFG, FG - Nil, RM - 2.

Please help.

Thanks in advance.

entry.title: Re: Are premium AI features (require SAP AI Units) availa...
entry.content:
To enable and be able to demo the embedded AI capabilities, I believe partners need to acquire SAP AI Units (SKU 8018592) from the SAP customer (Not TDD) price list. This is the same process partners use to purchase SAP software for their own productive use. 

Partners should contact their SAP Account Executives (AE) to purchase AI units, or alternatively use this link to contact SAP sales for a quote. 

When the SAP AI Units have been added to the partner’s entitlements (this can be checked in “SAP for Me”, under the “Finance & Legal Business AI” tab), the partner can activate the individual AI features for each individual SAP embedded AI feature activation instructions.

A little more color commentary... I would love to be contradicted on this (and learn that a TDD license is available for direct purchase by a partner), but I think unfortunately today a sales rep needs to be involved. By way of one data point We (a partner and I) went into Manage my Services to try to add a TDD SKU for AI Units but we did not find it. So if you are having the same experience (or different) let us know. Thanks!

entry.title: Re: Rule based automatic carrier determination in S/4HANA TM – clean-core approach
entry.content:
Hello Hari,  great post!!

One question from my side. You mentioned a few changes related to the UI.  Are these UI changes in the Transport Cockpit?

Thank you!

entry.title: Re: what is the retention period for the job logs in appl...
entry.content:
Hello,

In SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, the retention period for application job logs and spool outputs is 30 days by default. After that, the system automatically deletes the data and cannot extend it.

To retain results longer, you must download or export them before expiry.

Best regards,
Chuma

entry.title: Extension ledger with type "S" on the top "P" extension ledger.
entry.content:
Dear all,

I want to create extension ledger with type "S" on the top "P" extension ledger.
However, when I try to create an extension ledger with "S" type using the "Define Extension Ledger" configuration,
I cannot find an extension ledger with the "P" type in the Underlying ledger of an extension ledger and cannot specify it.
(Refer to attached file.)
As shown below, the SAP documentation on multiple extension ledgers says it's possible, but I'd like to know if it's not possible in the public cloud or if I'm misunderstanding something.

On top of an extension ledger with the standard journal entries type, you can create an extension ledger of any type.
On top of a type P extension ledger, you can create another type P or type S extension ledger.
On top of a type S extension ledger, you can only create another type S extension ledger.

Thank you,

Su young.

entry.title: Re: Depreciation calculation based on net book value
entry.content:
Hi Vijay,

Thanks for your quick reply.

I have checked with LINS depreciation key, but it is not working. Still it is taking the Acquisition value as base value for depreciation calculation and not considering the NBV which comes after two depreciation posting of first two periods.

Regards,

Amitava

entry.title: Re: Depreciation calculation based on net book value
entry.content:
@Walagrof,

Thanks for your quick help and suggestion.

1st solution

Its a workaround which we can do if there is no other way to achieve the solution. Business doesn't want to transfer the value to other asset.

2nd solution

AS81 is for Legacy asset transfer, which we can't use in this case.

We are trying to achieve this with Depreciation Key, ig this can be achieved with any specific Base method in Multi level method.

entry.title: ABAP2XLSX demo programs
entry.content:
Experts,

I am trying to install ABAP2XLSX demo programs in a local package. I am getting an error while trying to activate the programs. 

TYPE ZCL_EXCEL is unknown.

I have attached the error screenshot. I followed the steps in this link to setup ABAP GIT standalone program. 
Can you please let me know if I missed any step which is causing the issue. 

Thanks

Bharath

entry.title: Re: Extension ledger with type "S" on the top "P" extensi...
entry.content:
Hello dear user,

And thank you for asking your question in the SAP Community blog.

In SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, your observation is correct: you cannot create an extension ledger of type "S" on top of an extension ledger of type "P" even though the SAP documentation mentions this as theoretically possible in general.

 

Key Clarification

The SAP documentation you're referring to most likely reflects SAP S/4HANA On-Premise or Private Cloud Edition behavior. In S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition (2SL), the extensibility and ledger configuration capabilities are more restricted by design to ensure system standardization and reduce complexity in upgrades and maintenance.

 

Your Issue in Detail:

You said:

"I cannot find an extension ledger with the 'P' type in the Underlying ledger of an extension ledger..."

This is expected in Public Cloud, where:

  • The "Underlying Ledger" field only shows the standard ledger (type 0) or base extension ledger (type P) if SAP allows it.
  • SAP does not currently allow creating a chain of extension ledgers (i.e. P → S) in the Public Cloud Edition even if technically possible in the Private Cloud or On-Premise environments.

 

Official Restriction

SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition:

  • Allows creating one level of extension ledgers on top of the standard ledger.
  • Does not support chaining extension ledgers (P → S, P → P, S → S) in practice, even though documentation may suggest theoretical capability.

 

I hope this will help you answer your question.

Best regards,

Jeremy

entry.title: Re: Integrating SAP Ariba SLP with S/4HANA Public Cloud Using SAP Master Data Integration
entry.content:
Hi Ram, For partitioned supplier data, SAP recommends using the SAP MDI service alongside scope item 2NV. You can check the 2NV guide here.

This blog outlines different options, and the SAP Help documentation can be used to understand the integration procedure

entry.title: Re: ABAP2XLSX demo programs
entry.content:
Hi Baranth,

please check if you have installed ABAP2XLSX itself before installing the demo. Is the mentioned class ZCL_EXCEL present in your system (maybe it just needs activation)?

Have a look at this guide: https://abap2xlsx.github.io/abap2xlsx/abapGit-installation

entry.title: Re: S/4 HANA Private Cloud Extensibility - Manage Reserva...
entry.content: Thanks, for Business Context - Reservation Document Item I am unable to find BAdI where I can add value for custom fields for all rows shown in table control

entry.title: Re: Printing Adobe forms via local server or AWS
entry.content:
Hi @EmilioContreras 

You might have the bottleneck in the ABAP code or the ADS, which is Java. A little bit of information must be provided, in ABAP you can capture it with ST05 or SAT, I also like the STAT, if you are unfamiliar, ask your basis.

If it's on the ADS, tracing the Java is a little bit more tricky but it can be done, and ADS can be parallelized, look for note 2714231 and a java ADS parameter called "poolmax".

If the rest of your SAP servers are running on AWS, it does make sense to have the ADS running on AWS as well, yes

entry.title: Re: Integrating SAP Ariba SLP with S/4HANA Public Cloud Using SAP Master Data Integration
entry.content: Hi , but since we dont have Guided Buying is not enabled hence cannot integrate Partition supplier via MDI.

entry.title: Re: ABAP2XLSX demo programs
entry.content:
Hi M-K,

Thanks for your reply. I have installed ABAP2XLSX as a first step and then I am trying to install demo programs. I don't see attachment option while replying now. Not sure why. I wanted to share a screenshot which shows the objects in ABAP2XLSX. 

Let me type it out instead. I have below classes ZCL_ABAPGIT_FACTORY, ZCL_ABAPGIT_INJECTOR. I have these subpackages ZABAP2XLSX_UTILS and ZABAP2XLSX_EXITS. This is to give an idea that I have successfully installed ABAP2XLSX objects. Basically I downloaded the zip file for ABAP2XLSX github link. Using ABAP Git standalone program, I imported this zip file in my system which installed these objects. 

Class ZCL_TYPE does not exist in my system.

Thanks

Bharath

entry.title: KISS Your Complex AI Projects Goodbye
entry.content:
Another SAP Sapphire is in the books, and what a whirlwind of AI announcements it was! The annual conference wrapped up recently and was buzzing with excitement around artificial intelligence innovations across SAP's portfolio. From Joule's expanded capabilities to new partnerships with AI powerhouses, the message was clear: AI is transforming business, and SAP is at the forefront of this revolution.

The Rapid Evolution of Business AI

As I walked the show floor in Orlando, one thing became crystal clear – AI is no longer just a futuristic concept. It's here, it's evolving rapidly, and organizations are well-advised to think how this technology will transform their businesses. SAP's CEO Christian Klein emphasized this point during his keynote address, highlighting that we're "entering a new phase of AI with endless opportunities for all of us."

What struck me most was the sheer pace of innovation. The technology is advancing at breakneck speed (see a summary here), and businesses that don't start experimenting now risk being left behind.

"From humble beginnings come great things."

This ancient proverb perfectly captures where we stand with AI today. We're witnessing the early stages of a transformative technology that will reshape how businesses operate.

Our Journey with the SAP AppHaus

Amidst this AI revolution, our team at Syntax has been on our very own journey of exploration. Working closely with the SAP AppHaus team, we embarked on an adventure to understand how generative AI could solve real business challenges in a practical, approachable way.

Last year, this collaboration (read more) led to a significant milestone – the development of an AI-powered timesheet tracking.

This experience gave us valuable insights that we were thrilled to share during our presentation at Sapphire 2025, alongside Carlos Estala Velasco from the SAP AppHaus.

Simplicity by Design

For our presentation, we showcased our AI-powered time-tracking solution. In comparison to all the AI-centered announcements and demos during the keynotes, our solution may look a bit simple, and some people may cannot help it, but be reminded of Shania Twain's song: "That don't impress me much!"

At first glance, our demonstration might have seemed underwhelming – a simple use case (just a time-sheet entry solution), straightforward architecture, and a clean, minimalist interface (typical chatbot UX)

But that simplicity? It was entirely by design.

We deliberately embraced the KISS principle (Keep It Simple, Stupid) for several important reasons:

  1. Learning Focus: By keeping the business case straightforward, we could focus on learning the fundamentals of building AI solutions without getting bogged down in complex requirements.
  2. Clear Value Demonstration: The simplicity allowed us to clearly demonstrate the value of AI in a relatable context that everyone could understand. And our consultants love it, as it greatly speeds up this dull, repetitive process.
  3. Faster Iteration: A simpler solution meant we could iterate quickly, learning and adapting as we went along.
  4. Accessibility: The approachable nature of the project made it easier for team members with varying technical backgrounds to contribute and learn.

As Leonardo de Araujo, our SVP of Global SAP Innovation, noted during the presentation:
"As early adopters of SAP S/4HANA Cloud and participants in the GenAI Lighthouse program, we've used SAP Business AI to tackle industry challenges like inefficient timesheet processing. This innovation has the potential of significantly reducing our Days Sales Outstanding. Our estimates show we can save up to $100,000 per day of DSO reduction by shortening the billing cycle."

Key Takeaways: Start Small, Think Big

If there's one message we wanted attendees to take home from our presentation, it's this: don't wait for the perfect, complex use case to start your AI journey. Instead:

  1. Start Small: Begin with simple, focused use cases where AI can deliver clear value.
  2. Use Proper Methodology: Leverage structured approaches like those offered by the SAP AppHaus to identify and develop meaningful use cases.
  3. Foster an AI Culture: Encourage experimentation and learning throughout your organization.
  4. Look Beyond ERP: Many valuable AI use cases extend beyond traditional ERP boundaries.
  5. Embrace the Platform: SAP is building a robust AI foundation – leverage it as it continues to evolve.

The Path Forward

As we reflect on our AI journey so far, we're more convinced than ever that AI represents a transformative opportunity for businesses. But realizing this potential doesn't require massive, complex projects from the start. In fact, the opposite is often true – beginning with simple, focused applications allows organizations to build competence, confidence, and a foundation for more ambitious initiatives. As we did with progressing from our humble beginnings to launching our first SAP-certified GenAI offering: our Syntax AI CodeGenie solution, which helps SAP customers to document their custom code and interact with this documentation (e.g. to prepare for their S/4 migration.)

The SAP AppHaus offers an excellent starting point with their Business AI Explore Workshop, which helps identify meaningful use case opportunities, and their Business AI Design Workshop, which transforms ideas into feasible solution concepts.

In the words of Joyce Meyer, "Small beginnings are the launching pad to great endings."

Our simple timesheet tracking application may not have been the most impressive demo at Sapphire, but it represents something more important – a practical first step on what promises to be an extraordinary journey. As such, let me encourage all of you to take that first step and start exploring AI. Or even better, share your own story about the journey so far! I'd be happy to hear how you tackled it!

entry.title: how to add standard field min. shelf life expiration date in MM41/MM42/MM43
entry.content:
Hello SAP Experts,

I'm working with SAP Retail and trying to ensure that the standard field "Minimum Shelf Life Expiration Date" (e.g., MARC-MHDRZ) is not visible in the material master transactions MM41 (Create Article) and MM42 (Change Article). Screen sequence is already maintained and this is a standard field (not a custom one).

What I’ve Tried:
- Checked field selection groups in `OMS9`
- Verified Field Reference in `OMS2`
- The field is not hidden in configuration

Question:
How can I make this field appear in both MM41 and MM42?
Is this purely configuration-related or is screen enhancement (SE51 or BADI) required?

Any guidance or steps would be highly appreciated.

entry.title: Re: Crystal Report: Object Reference not set to an Instan...

entry.title: Re: Is there a way to reprice PO items in mass?
entry.content:
Hello @Yousssef123698745 

Yes, that’s correct. In SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, PO repricing is only possible per item, and only if no follow-on documents exist (like goods receipts or invoices).

There is currently no standard option to reprice POs in bulk, and no extensibility workaround due to cloud restrictions.

check this SAP Help Portal for Manage Purchase Orders.

With kind regards

Chuma

entry.title: FAIL: process hdbdaemon HDB Daemon not running
entry.content: i have error in my Hana S/4 Appliance server while starting it showsHDB daemon not working "assume that newDB is not runnig" error shows.

entry.title: SAP Mobile Start via SAP Start Booster – No Apps or Products Showing Post Setup (Foundation Plan)
entry.content:
Hi @sapcommunity  & @sapexperts 

We are currently working on enabling access to SAP S/4HANA Cloud Fiori applications via the SAP Mobile Start app, using the SAP Start booster in SAP BTP. However, we are facing an issue where no content appears on the SAP Start launchpad—even though the setup completes successfully.

Initial Approach – Manual Configuration

Initially, we tried to manually expose S/4HANA content to SAP Build Work Zone (Foundation Plan):

  • Verified that relevant business catalogs were marked as “Available for SAP Build Work Zone / SAP Start.”

  • Ensured that business roles were properly configured and exposed to SAP BTP.

Challenge:
Due to limitations of the Foundation Plan, we were unable to access the Content Manager or assign roles in the Site Directory, which prevented us from proceeding further with manual setup.

References Followed:
https://learning.sap.com/learning-journeys/Setting-Up-SAP-Build-Work-Zone-standard-edition-and-SAP-Mobile-Start-with-SAP-S-4HANA/importing-content-to-sap-build-work-zone-standard-edition-2-2

Alternative Approach – SAP Start Booster

To overcome these constraints, we executed the “Set Up SAP Start” Booster from SAP BTP. The booster ran successfully and completed the following:

  • ✅ Created the BTP subaccount, assigned entitlements, and configured trust.

  • ✅ Subscribed to SAP Build Work Zone – Foundation Plan.

  • ✅ Configured destinations to the SAP S/4HANA Cloud system.

  • ✅ Created the default SAP Start site.

  • ✅ Set up SAP Cloud Identity Services (IAS and IPS).

  • ✅ Integrated the S/4HANA Cloud system automatically.

As a result, the SAP Start site is now accessible via both desktop and the SAP Mobile Start app.

References Followed:
https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blog-posts-by-sap/setting-up-sap-start-with-sap-s-4hana-cloud-public-edition/ba-p/13573816

Current Issue

Despite the successful booster execution:

  • ⚠️ SAP Start site shows the message:
    “Currently, there aren’t any products for you. Please check back again later.”

  • ⚠️ No Fiori apps or S/4HANA content is visible in the launchpad.

Troubleshooting Performed

We have already validated the following:

  • 🔹 Trust configuration between BTP and IAS is correctly set.

  • 🔹 Users are provisioned and mapped properly via IPS.

  • 🔹 Exposed business catalogs and roles in S/4HANA.

  • 🔹 Booster completed without any reported errors.

  • 🔹 Attempted to recheck destinations and Channel Manager (though limited under Foundation Plan).

  • 🔹 Tried accessing SAP Start from both desktop and mobile, same result.

Request for Help / Clarifications

We’d appreciate your support with the following:

  1. Is there any missing step required to make the apps visible after the booster setup?

  2. Does the Foundation Plan impose any limitation that restricts content visibility in SAP Start?

  3. Would upgrading to SAP Build Work Zone – Standard Plan be required for full SAP Start functionality?

  4. Can anyone who successfully implemented SAP Mobile Start via Foundation Plan share insights?

Any pointers or confirmations from the SAP team or community experts would be greatly appreciated.

Thanks in advance!
Nithesh T S

entry.title: Re: How to Prepare for the SAP BTP Solution Architect Certification (with Sample Questions)
entry.content: Hi Amin - thank you so much for this guide!  I used it extensively to prepare for the exam.  The practice questions were spot on as well as the additional resources.  It took me a couple tries but I was successful in passing the exam and earning my accreditation in the end.  For anyone preparing for the test, this is the right place to go!

entry.title: Re: How to use Characteristic ID and Phrase Key to find l...
entry.content:
Hello

regarding the first part:

**Requirement:**

I only  the following input Characteristic ID (e.g.,ZSAP_EHS_9999_000_CATEGORY) and Phrase Key (e.g., CUST-TD-9999). 

Using only this information, I need to retrieve a list of Material Numbers (MATNR) that are linked to substances/specifications where the characteristic matches this phrase key using  BAPI_BUS1077_GETDETAIL

This is normally achieved by a real "user" /using SAP transaction CG02) and not via ABAP" Report; but via ABAP report it is in theory possible to achieve this.

EHS does have some "powerfull search options (please check SAP online help).. Assuming the following:

The material in scope should be assigned to a "REAL_SUB": by using the CG02 search options you can get a list of specifications having the needed data. On top: you can extract than the "materials" as assigned to the REAL_SUB

But by specifying a "material number" (as part of the research) in the so called "hit list" the material name is displayed.

In a nutshell:  Anyhow: the last step: here you can use then BAPI_BUS1077_GETDETAIL to read the data, For the other part: you nee to look for the function modules which are responsible for the "search". There is on function module used to search for if the input is only a "phrase key".  But i propose: To get  solution you should look for a functional EHS ressource and a technical resource which can help you to get the solution.

Wish you the best

C.B

entry.title: Currency exchange rate import using web service
entry.content:
Hello,

I'm trying to set up exchange rate import using available web service:

API I'm going to use for my test: 

https://api.unirateapi.com/api/rates?api_key=3dduQ18Xt2335GM3tJWEi8Qk1lKmfF60cyilrzx9wFqadR6kiAVSUasVr10CPWGZ&from=USD

I created project in SOAPUI with WSDL file I took from 'Communication Arrangements':

I used user id and password I set in 'Communication Arrangements' but getting the error you can see in screenshot above.

Not sure if I'm doing something wrong. Generally, I have lack of understanding how exchange rate taken from external provider API will be picked up by interface and uploaded to SAP ByD.

Any help much appreciated.

entry.title: Re: Content Enricher & Request Reply in sap cpi
entry.content: Good explanation

entry.title: Beginner’s Guide to SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud Test Automation for Functional Consultants and Testers
entry.content:
Introduction

In the world of SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud, change is constant—monthly delivery of innovations, localisation updates, and fast-paced deployments. Ensuring business continuity and quality assurance in such a dynamic environment is no longer optional—it's essential.

SAP’s Test Automation Tool (TAT), embedded within the S/4HANA Public Cloud system, empowers functional consultants and testers to build automated test cases without coding and execute them as part of release cycles or ongoing change initiatives.

In this post, I’ll walk you through what TAT is, why it matters, and how you can start automating your first business process—with no need to install anything externally.

What is the Test Automation Tool for SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud ?

SAP's built-in Test Automation Tool (TAT) is a cloud-native feature available for all S/4HANA Public Cloud customers. It's designed to automate business process testing without any third-party tools.

Key features include:

  • Pre-delivered test scripts for standard business processes (like Procure-to-Pay, Order-to-Cash)
  • A graphical UI to record and modify test steps
  • Support for reusable test components and variants
  • Integration with SAP Cloud ALM for test orchestration
  • Test analytics for tracking pass/fail results

Why Use TAT ?

Benefit

Description

Speed

Automate in hours what used to take days manually

Resilience

Standard test scripts get updated with SAP releases

Compliance

Easy to run tests before and after upgrades

Low-Code

Functional users can build and update tests without ABAP skills

Step-by-Step: How to Create Your First Test Script

Let’s say you want to automate a scenario - Procurement of Direct Materials in the system

🔹Step 1: Open the Test Automation Tool App

  • Launch the SAP Fiori Launchpad.
  • Open the Automated Testing space.
    • Pre-requisite: Ensure your user is assigned the role SAP_BR_ADMIN_TEST_AUTOMATION to access the test automation features.
  • Navigate to the “Manage Your Test Processes” app to start working with test scripts.

🔹Step 2: Choose a Pre-Delivered Test Script

  • Use the search function to locate a relevant SAP-delivered test script, such as:
    • “Procurement of Direct Materials” or “J45”
  • Review the process steps to ensure it matches your requirements.

🔹Step 3: Copy and Customise the Test Script

  • Click “Copy” to create your own Test Process as a custom version.
  • Modify the test process:
    • Adjust steps to suit your business scenario
    • Enter required input parameters (e.g., supplier, material number, plant, quantity)
  • Make the Test Script Visible.

🔹Step 4: Create a Test Plan

  • Open the “Test Your Processes” app.
  • Click the “+” (Create) button to define a new Test Plan.
  • Search and select your customised test script from Step 3.
  • Click “Save” to save your Test Plan.

🔹Step 5: Execute the Test

  • Open your saved Test Plan.
  • Click “Execute”.

🔹Step 6: Review Test Results

  • Go to the “Test Results” - > “Logs” section.
  • Review:
    • Pass/Fail status for each step
    • Execution logs for troubleshooting
    • Screenshots captured during test run for audit and validation

Best Practices for Success

✅ Use Pre-Delivered Scripts
Don't reinvent the wheel. Start from SAP-delivered templates and tailor them.

✅ Maintain Reusable Components
Create and reuse process steps and scripts

✅ Align with Release Cycles
Execute automation runs before and after innovation updates to catch regressions early.

✅ Leverage Cloud ALM
Integrate with Cloud ALM for full test orchestration, requirements traceability, and defect logging.

Conclusion

The SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud Test Automation Tool is not just a nice-to-have—it's your first line of defence against change-related defects in a fast-evolving cloud ERP world. Functional consultants and test engineers now have the power to automate business processes without writing a single line of code.

In future posts, I’ll deep-dive into:

  • Creating test data variants
  • Handling failed scripts

entry.title: Re: Depreciation calculation based on net book value

Hi @AmitavaSaha 

Did you check LINS depreciation key, it should have remaining useful life checked. Please check and make sure remaining useful life is checked for that particular depreciation key. 

 

Regards

Vijaykumar

entry.title: Re: Sap project Cooling period
entry.content:
Hi @CharanTeja3 

Success of SAP Project depends on your various factors. Below are some of the most important points -

  1. Successfully completion of User Acceptance Test.
  2. Successful Data Migration.
  3. Successful completion of first month end post GO Live.

 

Regards 

Vijaykumar

Click on "Paying Company Code"

This is where you can type in the jalwa game invite special code *536721303057*.

There's no penalty if you leave it blank, but it's easy to enter if you want to.

entry.title: Re: Migration for Asset Under Construction in S/4 HANA Public Cloud
entry.content: Dear Experts, Our fiscal year runs from April to March, and we are currently uploading the opening data for April. For Assets Under Construction (AUC), I used transaction type 900. While all other asset balances are correctly dated March 31, 2025, the AUC amount is recorded with the date April 1, 2025. I also migrated the GL opening balance dated March 31, 2025. However, when I checked the trial balance as of March 31, 2025, the AUC amount does not appear. It only shows up when I run the trial balance as of April 1, 2025. This causes the GL opening balance not to match. Has anyone encountered this issue? How did you resolve it? Could you please advise on the correct approach? Thank you in advance!

entry.title: PREDICTOKEN: Fast, Local Token Count Prediction for ABAP and Edge-Devices
entry.content:
Hello, my name is Alice, and I've been working with ABAP/SAP for about two decades with a lifelong passion for performance optimization. Today I want to share a technical solution I developed about a year ago when LLM API costs were significantly higher and token prediction was even more crucial than today.

Let me show you 😃

TL;DR:

GitHub: oisee/zllm (see ZCL_LLM_00_PREDICTOKEN)
The Problem: How to predict token counts without calling expensive tokenizer APIs
The Solution: Linear regression trained on ABAP corpus, generating pure ABAP prediction code
The Innovation: Machine learning model deployment in environments with zero native ML libraries
Use Cases: SAP/ABAP systems, edge AI, air-gapped environments, cost-sensitive pipelines

What Problem Are We Trying to Solve?

When building LLM integrations, especially in enterprise environments, you constantly face this dilemma:

"How many tokens will this text consume?"

The traditional approaches all have significant drawbacks:

  1. Call the tokenizer API first - Adds 200-500ms latency + network overhead + additional cost
  2. Conservative estimation - Wastes 30-50% of available context window
  3. Character-based approximation - Wildly inaccurate (can be off by 40%+)
  4. Send and pray - Results in 10-15% failed requests and wasted API calls

This was especially painful ~1 year ago when:

  • LLM API costs were significantly higher
  • Every failed request due to token limits was expensive
  • Context windows were smaller, making efficiency crucial
  • Enterprise budgets for AI experimentation were tighter

For ABAP environments, this problem is amplified because there are no native tokenizer libraries. You can't just pip install tiktoken - you're stuck with external API calls for everything.

Why This Matters Beyond ABAP

While I built this for SAP systems, the core challenge applies everywhere:

Edge AI devices - Limited connectivity, need local token prediction
Cost-sensitive environments - Every API call matters
Real-time systems - Can't afford tokenizer API latency
Air-gapped systems - No external API access allowed
Legacy environments - No modern ML libraries available

The technique is portable. As long as your environment supports basic string operations and math, this can be deployed without ML runtimes or external APIs.

The fundamental question: Can you predict token counts using only basic math and text processing?

Validation: GOOD ENOUGH

The practicality of PREDICTOKEN is validated based on these criteria:

Accuracy: Prediction error should be < 10% for typical text (< 5% for code)
Performance: Prediction time should be negligible (< 1ms)
Overestimation Bias: Slight intentional overestimation (~2-3%) is built-in to avoid payload cutoff errors
Zero Dependencies: Must work in any environment with basic arithmetic

These criteria ensure the system provides practical value without requiring external services or complex dependencies.

Understanding the Data

Let's examine what we're predicting:

Input: Any text (ABAP code, documentation, mixed content)
Output: Token count for specific LLM tokenizers (GPT, Mistral, etc.)
Challenge: Different tokenizers produce different counts for identical input

Example with ABAP code:

" This ABAP code snippet:
CLASS lcl_ DEFINITION DEFERRED.

CLASS zcl_llm_00_predictoken DEFINITION LOCAL FRIENDS lcl_.

CLASS lcl_ DEFINITION FOR TESTING
 RISK LEVEL HARMLESS
 DURATION SHORT.
 PRIVATE SECTION.
 TYPES: string_t TYPE zif_llm_00_types=>string_t.
 DATA: mo_cut TYPE REF TO zcl_llm_00_predictoken.
 CLASS-DATA: gs_coeff TYPE zcl_llm_00_predictoken=>ts_coeff.
 METHODS: setup,
 test_constructor FOR TESTING,
 test_class_constructor FOR TESTING,
 new_instance_creation FOR TESTING,
 new_for_model_instance FOR TESTING,
 predict_single FOR TESTING,
 predict_for_itab FOR TESTING,
 extract_features FOR TESTING,
 minimal_prediction FOR TESTING.

 DATA: mv_in TYPE string.
ENDCLASS.

CLASS lcl_ IMPLEMENTATION.
 METHOD setup.
 mo_cut = zcl_llm_00_predictoken=>new( ).
 DO 84 TIMES.
 mv_in = mv_in && `This is a test. `.
 ENDDO.
 ENDMETHOD.
 ....
ENDCLASS.

GPT-4 Tokenizer: ~255 tokens
Mistral Tokenizer: ~257 tokens
Character Count: 946 characters
Word Count: 90 words

Simple character/word ratios would give you estimates between 20-40 tokens - not precise enough for reliable payload sizing.

The Machine Learning Approach

Instead of trying to reverse-engineer complex tokenizer algorithms, I took a feature-based regression approach:

Feature Engineering

TYPES:
  BEGIN OF ts_features,
    text_length  TYPE i,     " Total character count
    words        TYPE i,     " Word boundaries (\w+'*\w*|\w)
    punctuations TYPE i,     " [.,;:!?'"] count
    numbers      TYPE i,     " \d count  
    whitespaces  TYPE i,     " \s count
    lines        TYPE i,     " Newline count + 1
    sentences    TYPE i,     " [.!?]\s* count + 1
  END OF ts_features.
 

These features capture the linguistic patterns that correlate with tokenization behavior:

  • Text length: Base correlation with token count
  • Words: Primary tokenization units
  • Punctuation: Often becomes separate tokens
  • Numbers: Special tokenization rules
  • Whitespace: Token boundary indicators
  • Lines: Important for code structure
  • Sentences: Semantic boundaries matter

Training Pipeline

The training happens in Python, but deployment is pure ABAP:

  1. Corpus Collection: Collected diverse ABAP code samples and documentation
  2. Ground Truth Generation: Called actual tokenizer APIs to get true counts
  3. Feature Extraction: Computed the 7 features for each sample
  4. Linear Regression: Trained separate models per tokenizer
  5. Coefficient Export: Generated ABAP constants from Python coefficients

The key insight: Linear regression is simple enough to implement anywhere.

Implementation: From Python Training to ABAP Deployment

Here's the beautiful part. Python training produces these regression coefficients:

# GPT-4 Model Training Results:
# Coefficients: [ 0.0610605   0.5011021   0.93815106  1.16759457 
#                -0.01792212  4.63131677 -1.67103032]
# Intercept: -28.82816309097143
# R²: 0.987 (excellent fit)

# Mistral Model Training Results:
# Coefficients: [ 0.18695786 -0.28615407  0.52599872  1.80894008 
#                -0.02379413  5.65761217  0.34037584]
# Intercept: -56.52809407193536
# R²: 0.981 (excellent fit)
 

Which automatically generate ABAP constants:

METHOD class_constructor.
  " Auto-generated from Python training
  gs_co_gpt = VALUE #(
    text_length  = '0.0610605'
    words        = '0.5011021'
    punctuations = '0.93815106'
    numbers      = '1.16759457'
    whitespaces  = '-0.01792212'
    lines        = '4.63131677'
    sentences    = '-1.67103032'
    intercept    = '-28.82816309097143'
  ).
  
  gs_co_mistral = VALUE #(
    text_length  = '0.18695786'
    words        = '-0.28615407'
    punctuations = '0.52599872'
    numbers      = '1.80894008'
    whitespaces  = '-0.02379413'
    lines        = '5.65761217'
    sentences    = '0.34037584'
    intercept    = '-56.52809407193536'
  ).
ENDMETHOD.
 

Real-Time Prediction: Seven Operations

The actual prediction is remarkably simple:

METHOD predict_tokens.
  DATA lv_sum TYPE decfloat34.
  
  " Linear regression formula
  lv_sum = ceil(
    ms_-intercept +
    ms_-text_length  * is_-text_length +
    ms_-words        * is_-words +
    ms_-punctuations * is_-punctuations +
    ms_-numbers      * is_-numbers +
    ms_-whitespaces  * is_-whitespaces +
    ms_-lines        * is_-lines +
    ms_-sentences    * is_-sentences
  ).
  
  " Ensure minimum reasonable prediction
  rv_ = COND #(
    WHEN lv_sum > mv_min THEN lv_sum
    ELSE mv_min
  ).
ENDMETHOD.
 

Seven multiplications, seven additions, one ceiling operation. That's it.

Execution time: < 1ms on any modern system.

Feature Extraction: The Real Work

The computational work happens in feature extraction:

METHOD extract_features.
  rs_-text_length = strlen( iv_ ).
  
  " Word Count using regex
  DATA word_regex TYPE string VALUE `(\w+'*\w*|\w)`.
  rs_-words = count( val = iv_ regex = word_regex ).
  
  " Punctuation Count  
  DATA punc_regex TYPE string VALUE `[.,;:!?'"]`.
  rs_-punctuations = count( val = iv_ regex = punc_regex ).
  
  " Number Count
  DATA number_regex TYPE string VALUE `\d`.
  rs_-numbers = count( val = iv_ regex = number_regex ).
  
  " Whitespace Count
  DATA whitespace_regex TYPE string VALUE `\s`.
  rs_-whitespaces = count( val = iv_ regex = whitespace_regex ).
  
  " Line count
  rs_-lines = count( val = iv_ sub = cl_abap_char_utilities=>newline ) + 1.
  
  " Sentence Count
  DATA sentence_regex TYPE string VALUE `(\.s*|\?s*|!s*)`.
  rs_-sentences = count( val = iv_ regex = sentence_regex ) + 1.
ENDMETHOD.
 

Even this is fast - regex operations complete in microseconds for typical text sizes.

Why Linear Regression Works

You might wonder: "Why not use a neural network or transformer?"

For token count prediction, linear regression has unique advantages:

  1. Deployable Anywhere: No ML runtime required
  2. Interpretable: You can see which features matter most
  3. Fast: Constant-time prediction regardless of text length
  4. Reliable: No black-box failures or edge cases
  5. Small: Seven coefficients vs millions of parameters

The R² values (0.987 for GPT, 0.981 for Mistral) show that linear relationships capture most of the variance in token counts. While not perfect on edge cases (e.g., emojis, code-mixed text), it achieves excellent accuracy on structured and typical enterprise inputs like ABAP and documentation.

Model Adaptation Strategy

The system supports multiple tokenizers through a simple factory pattern:

" Get predictor for specific model
DATA(lo_predictor) = zcl_llm_00_predictoken=>new_for_model_type( 
  iv_ = zcl_llm_00_predictoken=>gc_llm_type-gpt 
).

" Or auto-detect from LLM configuration  
DATA(lo_predictor) = zcl_llm_00_predictoken=>new_for_llm( io_llm ).

" Make prediction
DATA(lv_tokens) = lo_predictor->predict( lv_text ).
 

Adding support for new tokenizers requires:

  1. Collect training samples
  2. Run Python regression training
  3. Add coefficients to ABAP constants
  4. Update factory method

Practical Applications

This approach enables several enterprise AI patterns:

Smart Context Management: Predict if additional context will fit
Batch Optimization: Group requests optimally without exceeding limits
Cost Estimation: Calculate API costs before making calls
Fallback Strategies: Switch to smaller models when content is too large
Performance Optimization: Avoid expensive tokenizer API calls

For edge AI or resource-constrained environments, this pattern could be even more valuable than in traditional cloud setups.

Stats and Performance

Accuracy:

  • GPT models: < 5% error on 95% of samples
  • Mistral models: < 7% error on 95% of samples
  • Overestimation bias: 2-3% (intentional safety margin)

Performance:

  • Feature extraction: ~10-50μs (depending on text size)
  • Prediction calculation: ~1μs
  • Total overhead: Negligible compared to any network operation

Memory footprint:

  • Model coefficients: ~200 bytes
  • Runtime overhead: Minimal (no caching needed)

Future Enhancements

The framework could be extended for:

Multi-language support: Train on different programming languages
Domain-specific models: Optimize for documentation vs code vs data
Dynamic retraining: Update coefficients based on new tokenizer versions
Ensemble methods: Combine multiple predictors for better accuracy

Why This Matters

PREDICTOKEN demonstrates a broader principle: You don't always need complex ML infrastructure to solve ML problems.

Sometimes the right answer is:

  • Train sophisticated models where you have resources
  • Deploy simple models where you need reliability
  • Bridge the gap with smart feature engineering

For enterprise environments with strict dependencies, air-gapped systems, or edge devices, this pattern of "train complex, deploy simple" could unlock many AI capabilities that would otherwise be impossible.

Conclusion

PREDICTOKEN shows that effective AI integration doesn't require cutting-edge infrastructure everywhere. By training regression models offline and deploying them as simple arithmetic, you can bring intelligent token prediction to any environment.

Whether you're working with ABAP, embedded systems, legacy platforms, or just want to minimize API dependencies, the principle remains: smart feature engineering + simple models can solve real problems.

While this module is part of the larger ZLLM framework (to be released), it's designed to run standalone. The code is available as part of the ZLLM framework. Feel free to adapt the approach for your own tokenizer prediction challenges!

About the Author:

Alice Vinogradova is a Senior Software Engineer with two decades of ABAP/SAP experience and a passion for bridging traditional enterprise systems with modern AI capabilities. She specializes in performance optimization and practical AI integration patterns for conservative enterprise environments.

P.S.

(Brought to you by me in my spare time over weekends, my personal NetWeaver 2022 (Docker) SAP S/4HANA instance, and a couple of imaginary friends: Claude and ChatGPT 😃

This continues my weekend exploration of enterprise AI in ABAP—previously I built ZVDB, a pure ABAP vector database that achieved remarkable performance without external dependencies.

I originally wanted to release ZLLM about a year ago, but it always seemed "not good enough" for public eyes. Despite many weekend attempts to clean it up for release, I kept preferring to "just improve it a bit and add more features"—the classic developer perfectionism trap 😃

So now I've decided to break the cycle: release ZLLM incrementally by submodules in the oisee/zllm repository, gradually filling the gaps. Better to share working solutions than perfect ones that never see daylight!

entry.title: batch determination for same material code into 2 line item in outbound delivery
entry.content:
When create outbound delivery for material but into 2 line item same batch determined for both line item even that the  quantity fully consumed for 1st line item.
All working fine for auto batch or batch spilt if the material not repeated in same delivery document.
all configation for auto batch determination is maintained.

1st line item batch 2

2nd line item batch

avalible quantity

if there's a standard or enhancement can fix this please to share .

Thanks in advance.
SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition

This is where you can type in the bdg invitation special code*5661713781304*.

There's no penalty if you leave it blank, but it's easy to enter if you want to.

entry.title: Material blocking for billing in SAP S/4 Public
entry.content:
Hello,

Business request is to block specific material type for billing.  Request is to put block for billing in material master. Which item category group of material to use for restriction in business scenario when material must be delivered but not invoiced?

Thanks, Aniram

entry.title: Blog series on my CPI + Camel learning journey - Interlude2 APIM debugging
entry.content:
Motivation

Well, as with MPL you have to start doing some real life debugging to figure out the tools you are provided with "have some space for improvement", to put it mildly.

And this is what happened to me when I had a weird case for one of my api proxies.

A little bit about Apigee and debug session structure

Not a secret that under the hood APIM has apigee platform (that google bought)

So, it turns out it was not SAP to blame for this "debug session" logic, but still the app they made is something special I have to say..

What bothers us is this stuff  https://cloud.google.com/apigee/docs/api-platform/debug/trace 

And particularly the structure of session/transactions https://cloud.google.com/apigee/docs/api-platform/debug/trace#download-structure

Without going into details (after all this blog does not aim to provide the full schema details) let's see what could be of our most interest here.

First, the whole session is an array of entries (we will call it transactions which are api calls)

Below we would see "point" array - kinda Steps of flow execution each having an array of "results" in turn.

[
    {
        "completed": true,
        "point": [
            {
                "id": "Paused|Resumed|StateChange|FlowInfo|Condition|Execution|Error",
                "results": [
                    {
                        "ActionResult": "DebugInfo|RequestMessage|ResponseMessage|ErrorMessage|VariableAccess"
                    }
                ]
            }
        ]
    }
]

These results contain some more details (which we see in the standard app as lists/forms) of key-value pairs

What is different is just name of "aggregation" - path in json to those arrays (eg "properties/property" or "headers" or "accessList")

{
    "ActionResult": "DebugInfo",
    "properties": {
        "property": [ { "name": "<PROP_NAME>", "value": "<PROP_VALUE>" } ]
    },
    "timestamp": "04-06-25 13:28:52:904" // yes, not a proper iso8601 timestamp ;(
}

{
    "ActionResult": "RequestMessage",
    "content": "<REQUEST_BODY_IF_PRESENT>",
    "headers": [ { "name": "<HEADER_NAME>", "value": "<HEADER_VALUE>" } ],
    "uRI": "<URI>",
    "verb": "<VERB>"
}

{
    "ActionResult": "ResponseMessage",
    "content": "<RESPONSE_BODY_TRIMMED>.....",
    "headers": [ { "name": "<HEADER_NAME>", "value": "<HEADER_VALUE>" } ],
    "reasonPhrase": "<STATUS_PHRASE>",
    "statusCode": "<STATUS_CODE>"
}

{
    "ActionResult": "ErrorMessage",
    "content": "<APIM_ERR_BODY>",
    "headers": [ { "name": "<HEADER_NAME>", "value": "<HEADER_VALUE>" } ],
    "reasonPhrase": "<STATUS_PHRASE>",
    "statusCode": "<STATUS_CODE>"
}

{
    "ActionResult": "VariableAccess",
    "accessList": [
        {
            "Get": { "name": "<VAR>", "value": "<VALUE_IF_GOT>" }
        },
        {
            "Set": { "name": "<VAR>", "success": true, "value": "<VALUE>" }
        }
    ]
}

Api Proxy debug viewer app

But most important thing is that we DO HAVE Request and Response (and Error) Method, Url, Content and Status!!

And so I decided to make a really simple frontend-only ui5 app based on Flexible Column Layout (which  is hidden behind the intent #apim-dbg in capic launchpad)

For this I just had to figure out the structure of the session and then apply some transformations to display it nicely.

And while the original app tries to follow "visual approach" apigee had in their app (you could see  it following the link above) - using lots of pictograms/icons while doing its best to hide essential information from us like target endpoint http code/status or request/response body - I decided to display our stuff via lists and forms, cuz as a developer I LOVE TEXT (which can even be searchable).

This image from apigee documentation illustrates it well https://docs.apigee.com/api-platform/fundamentals/what-are-flows (not 100% percent sure I am right regarding mapping of stages, but it somewhat makes sense to me)

Basically, the app displays our debugging session in the following way (kinda 5 levels):

  • Master - 1 Transactions (api calls) as Items // with objectstatus stuff
  • Detail - 2 Stages (list grouping) + 3 points (steps) as Items // also with some color coding
  • Detail-Detail - 4 ActionResults (form container) + 5 results params  as form elements

Some other things to point out here (at Detail screen): 

  • StateChange typically has RequestMessage|ResponseMessage|ErrorMessage ActionResult object appearing
It comes first in group and it is marked with blue highlight (also from this guy I calculated groups for detail list)
  • Error has some error information that doesn't help much but is a place in flow where error occured - marked red
  • Execution - is an interesting step, basically, this is where our policies run - it is marked amber
Properties of DebugInfo action here contain "type" or "stepDefinition-type" which is policy type (not 100% sure) and "
stepDefinition-name" or "stepDefinition-displayName" which is actual policy name as we created it.
I use it as a name for step instead of just "Execution" (this is why you don't see it in UI)
 
Here  are the screenshots of a simple debugging session that hopefully will immediately make sense to you

 

Some JS magic

Ok, this might even look good (or at least better than the original), but how to get the data?

Well, it turns out, SAP did a great job exposing some global methods (and even oapiproxyData object ) in their app (yes it seems to be a really, really old one), so we can just programmatically create us a session and then fetch data into clipboard.

And for that I used super old trick: javascript favourites snippets (even had to google they were called "bookmarklets")

So, to start session you might want to use something like this:

sap.apimgmt.commons.ajaxHandler({
  data: JSON.stringify({"name": oapiproxyData.name, "version": oapiproxyData.version, "session_id":"pwn3d"}),
  headers: {"Content-Type": "application/json", "Accept": 'application/json', "operation": "start"},
  url: APIMGMT.TRACE_WORKSPACE_ROOT,
  type: APIMGMT.HTTP_METHODS.POST,
  success: console.log, error: console.log
})

 And to fetch data into your browser clipboard (and I was surprised I did not need a focusable element when using the bookmarkelt to write to it) you would do this:

sap.apimgmt.commons.ajaxHandler({
  data: JSON.stringify({"name": oapiproxyData.name, "version": oapiproxyData.version, "session_id":"pwn3d"}),
  headers: {"Content-Type": "application/json", "Accept": 'application/json', "operation": "get"},
  url: APIMGMT.TRACE_WORKSPACE_ROOT,
  type: APIMGMT.HTTP_METHODS.POST,
  success: function(data){ navigator.clipboard.writeText(JSON.stringify(data))}, error: console.log
})

 And I am not going to teach you how to add that stuff into bookmarks but wiki probably could.

Demo

In this demo we are going to reproduce the session from screenshots above.

For this purpose we use simplest api proxy possible - to anonymous Northwind service https://services.odata.org/V2/Northwind/Northwind.svc/ and protect it with apikey, so you can easily try it yourself in like 10 mins.

00:00 Intro and motivation

01:39 Northwind api proxy and debug with standard app (2 positive and 2 negative responses)

03:45 Try interpreting the results

04:58 But actual session data json contains more data and that is what we cover in blog (kind of)

06:06 A little bit about apigee stuff and json structure and corresponding app screens

08:34 How to obtain session data json (other than by copying from network requests)

10:08 Repeat same api test/debug routine with bookmarklets magic

11:36 Fetch session json data to finally see the new app in action (and try to interpret data there)

15:15 Confirm that AnalyticsPublisher policy/step indeed contains response message.status.code, request.path and request.verb as vars (but not body though)

16:10 "And we do see that we do have body which is very nice" (in proper place)

17:07 Final words about app screens and features and outro

P.S Disclaimer and feature requests/fixes

This is the simplest and mot naive implementation of  this app made out offrustration, so if for some reason you might want to test it there can (or must) be scenarios it does not support (haven't tried using scripts, conditional flows etc).

Please feel free to comment here regarding errors/features you might need, but DON'T paste session info here as it contains sensitive information about your apim instance.

entry.title: Re: SAP Stammtisch Zürich
entry.content: Today I met with Łukasz for a micro-Stammtisch in Brasserie Federal 🍺

entry.title: Re: batch determination for same material code into 2 lin...
entry.content: Kindly make sure correct availability check maintained in material master Sales: general/ plat data. also check checking rule assigned to availability check.

entry.title: Re: Create Outbound IDoc with Serialization
entry.content:
Hi,

I have exactly the same scenario. Did you find any solution for the above? 

Thanks.

entry.title: Re: Material blocking for billing in SAP S/4 Public
entry.content:
Billing block can be maintained at

1.Sales Order level

2.Delivery level 

3.Billing Document level

You can do enhancement to check material type of the line item material and set the billing block in sales order or at the time of Delivery creation.

entry.title: Re: Microsoft Azure Sentinel for SAP Solutions for SAP S/4 HANA 2020 FPS02
entry.content: Very well written guide. The company I work for will configure Microsoft Sentinel and needed something to get an idea.

entry.title: Re: Building Robust Enterprise Architecture: Unlocking Value Streams (In Business Context)
entry.content: @JansiMurugesan Thank you so much for your post! Where can I find the complete list of value streams? Also is it by Industry? e.g. SAP Utilities. Retail etc.? From what I have seen there do not appear to be industry flavors.

entry.title: Re: "PD Where Used" Button – A Chrome Extension for SAP Integration Suite
entry.content:
Hi Emre, thanks for sharing the useful extension. Is it also available in MS Edge? I observe that many companies now are prohibiting add-ons/extension in Chrome.

Sunny

entry.title: I am having issue when trying to initiate Service Instance for my practice system SD400
entry.content:
I am having issue when trying to initiate Service Instance for my practice system SD400.

I have followed attached instructions for creating ABAP Cloud Project in ADT, entered given service key, used provide link and opened new browser session, properly logged in and was authenticated -> but system keep prompting me to log on again and again. I attached the log on screenshot.

entry.title: Re: Understanding ABAP Pipeline Engine used for Data Integration in Datasphere
entry.content: Great job!!
